name: Roasted Tomatillo and Avocado Salsa (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 1 ear of fresh corn, kernels removed and blanched in boiling water for 30-seconds
1 jalapeno pepper or serrano pepper -- seeded and minced
1 medium avocado, halved, pitted and diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a cast-iron pan or ridged grill over medium-high heat for 15 minutes.
2. Add the tomatillos and cook, shaking the pan occasionally, until golden on all sides and soft, about 20 minutes. Remove from the pan and let cool completely.
3. Place the cooled tomatillos in a food processor and process until almost smooth.
4. In a large bowl, mix together the processed tomatillos, cilantro, garlic, red onion, corn kernels, lime juice, and jalapeno or serrano pepper (seeded and minced).
5. Add the diced avocado and stir gently. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Makes about 2-1/2 cups.
7. This salsa pairs well with simple seafood dishes, quesadillas, burritos, or tostadas. If fresh tomatillos are unavailable, substitute two 12-ounce cans, drained and chopped. Enjoy this salsa the same day it is made.
